context: Tests whether the agent writes syntactically correct JSONPath filter expressions wrapped in $.[?(...)] with the right operators for each use case.

checklist.name: All filters wrapped in $.[?(...)]
checklist.description: Every filter expression starts with $.[?( and closes with )]

checklist.name: tenantOnly uses regex with /i
checklist.description: tenantOnly uses the =~ operator with a regex literal anchored to t42. (e.g., /^t42\./i) including the i flag for case-insensitivity

checklist.name: highPriorityChat combines && correctly
checklist.description: highPriorityChat ANDs (using &&) a regex match on @.channel against chat. and an equality check on @.message.priority == 'high'

checklist.name: excludeSystem uses !
checklist.description: excludeSystem uses the negation operator ! around a predicate testing @.message.system == true (e.g., $.[?(!(@.message.system == true))])

checklist.name: botOrAdmin uses in [...]
checklist.description: botOrAdmin uses the in operator with an array literal containing the three user ids on the @.uuid field

checklist.name: References @ for current node
checklist.description: All filters use @ (e.g., @.channel, @.message, @.meta, @.uuid) to address fields of the current event, not $.

checklist.name: Filters target correct fields
checklist.description: Each filter uses the documented path: @.channel for channel name, @.message for publish payload fields, @.meta for publish metadata fields, @.uuid for publisher

checklist.name: NOTES mentions silent drop
checklist.description: Exported NOTES string explicitly mentions that wrong JSONPath syntax silently drops every event with no error
